metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_5354
metadata.comment:
Rationale: ORP2 knockdown causes a decrease of Ca2+ influx signals in a cholesterol-dependent manner.

"Initially, we examined the general presynaptic localization (VGlut1) of the voltage-gated Ca2+ channel CaV2.1, which was found unchanged in ORP2 KD hippocampal neurons (Figure 5A). Next, we investigated presynaptic Ca2+ influx by fluorescence live cell imaging of SynGCamp6f, a fast Ca2+ sensor fused to synaptophysin and thus located to SVs.46 Compared to control conditions, Ca2+ influx signals were reduced in ORP2 KD hippocampal neurons (Figures 5B and 5C).

The relative reduction of Ca2+ influx in ORP2 KD hippocampal neurons was especially apparent at stimulations with single (64.9% reduction; 0.35 ± 0.07 sc vs. 0.12 ± 0.03 ORP2 KD) or double (49.9% reduction; 0.65 ± 0.09 sc vs. 0.32 ± 0.05 ORP2 KD) APs (Figure 5B, 1 AP and 2 AP). Of note, the decrease in Ca2+ influx was more profound than what could be expected from the roughly 16% reduction of SVs assessed by electron microscopy (Figure 2H). This particularly visible impact of ORP2 KD at single and double APs indicates impaired Ca2+ channel activation and/or function.". Experimental description: M&M: "Experimental models: Organisms/strains - Mouse: C57/BL6N (WT)"

shRNA knockdown;
f(U6)snrw-ORP2 shRNA-1, Weber-Boyvat et al., (21) (PMID:32734583)
